overview: The bpf-next mailing list saw 47 patches across 14 series on June 22–23, with several significant features in late-stage review. The headline work is Leon Hwang's v7 series introducing first-class global per-CPU data support in BPF, complete with libbpf, bpftool, and bpf_iter integration. New kfuncs continue to grow the library: Mahe Tardy's bpf_icmp_send (v8) enables BPF programs to generate ICMP packets, while Longjun Tang proposes bpf_memcmp and bpf_strpbrk string helpers.

patches.title: bpf: Introduce global percpu data
patches.author: Leon Hwang
patches.summary: This is the core patch in an 11-patch v7 series that adds native global per-CPU data support to BPF programs, using a new BPF_ADDR_PERCPU instruction class. Programs can now declare per-CPU global variables directly in their ELF sections and have the verifier track them correctly, without resorting to per-CPU array maps as a workaround. The series also disallows interpreter fallback for the new instruction, adds libbpf feature probing and skeleton generation in bpftool, and includes comprehensive selftests covering read-only access, verifier log output, and iteration via bpf_iter. At v7, this long-running feature series appears close to landing.

patches.title: bpf: add bpf_icmp_send kfunc
patches.author: Mahe Tardy
patches.summary: Adds a new bpf_icmp_send kfunc that allows BPF programs attached at TC or cgroup_skb hooks to generate ICMP (and ICMPv6) error packets in response to incoming traffic. The series refactors a netfilter helper (nf_reject_fill_skb_dst) into the core IPv4 and IPv6 stacks so the kfunc can reuse it without depending on the netfilter subsystem. Now at v8, the series includes cgroup_skb tests for both IPv4 and IPv6, tc tests, and a recursion guard test to prevent reentrancy issues.

patches.title: bpf: add bpf_memcmp kfunc
patches.author: Longjun Tang
patches.summary: Proposes two new string/memory kfuncs: bpf_memcmp for comparing memory regions and bpf_strpbrk for locating the first occurrence of any character from a set within a string. These fill gaps in the BPF kfunc library for string-processing programs that currently have no equivalent to the standard C library functions. The series renames an existing selftest-local bpf_memcmp stub to avoid symbol conflicts, then adds both kfuncs with corresponding selftests.

patches.title: bpf: add BPF_F_COMPARE flag and compare fields to map elem UAPI
patches.author: Gyutae Bae
patches.summary: An RFC series proposing a compare-and-delete atomic operation for BPF hash maps, analogous to compare-and-swap but for deletion. A new BPF_F_COMPARE flag on BPF_MAP_DELETE_ELEM makes the kernel compare the stored value against a caller-supplied expected value before deleting, returning -EAGAIN if they differ. This allows userspace to safely delete map entries without races against concurrent updaters. The series adds the UAPI changes, implements the logic for BPF_MAP_TYPE_HASH, and includes selftests.

patches.title: bpf: Add BPF_FIB_LOOKUP_VLAN flag to bpf_fib_lookup() helper
patches.author: Avinash Duduskar
patches.summary: Extends the bpf_fib_lookup() helper with bidirectional VLAN awareness via two new flags. BPF_FIB_LOOKUP_VLAN instructs the helper to tag the outgoing packet with the VLAN ID found in the FIB lookup result, while BPF_FIB_LOOKUP_VLAN_INPUT tells it to use the VLAN ID from the incoming packet when performing the lookup. Together these allow BPF-based forwarding planes to handle VLAN-tagged traffic without additional post-lookup fixups. The v4 series adds selftests covering both flags.

patches.title: bpf: Mask pseudo pointer values in verifier logs
patches.author: Nuoqi Gui
patches.summary: Fixes a potential information leak in the BPF verifier's log output where pseudo-BTF ksym pointer values (raw kernel addresses) could appear unredacted in the verifier log visible to unprivileged users. The patch masks these values in the log using existing kernel address masking facilities. The v2 series includes a selftest that verifies the masking behavior for the pseudo-BTF ksym log path.

patches.title: bpf: support shift operations with non-const src operand
patches.author: Tianci Cao
patches.summary: Extends the BPF verifier to handle shift instructions (LSH, RSH, ARSH) where the shift amount register has a bounded but non-constant value, rather than rejecting such programs outright. When the verifier can prove the shift amount lies within a known range, it now propagates tighter bounds on the destination register instead of conservatively widening to the full 64-bit range. The accompanying selftest covers a variety of bounded-shift combinations to exercise the new reasoning.

patches.title: HID: bpf: Fix hid_bpf_get_data() range check
patches.author: Yiyang Chen
patches.summary: Corrects an off-by-one or overflow bug in the range check inside hid_bpf_get_data(), which could allow a BPF HID program to read beyond the end of the HID report buffer when a large size argument is supplied. The v4 series also adds a selftest helper that loads only the struct_ops maps relevant to a given test rather than all maps in the BPF object, reducing test overhead. A dedicated selftest then exercises the size-overflow path to prevent regressions.
